scope_note: QA CM Form Identification cert [399]. Primary sources: Hecke (1920) 'Eine neue Art von Zetafunktionen und ihre Beziehungen zur Verteilung der Primzahlen' Mathematische Zeitschrift 6:11-51 DOI: doi.org/10.1007/BF01458074 (Hecke Grossencharacters; Hecke L-functions; CM eigenvalues); Shimura (1971) 'Introduction to the Arithmetic Theory of Automorphic Functions' Princeton University Press ISBN 978-0-691-08092-5 (CM lifting; eigenvalues of Hecke operators for CM forms; level-conductor formula); Neukirch (1999) 'Algebraic Number Theory' Springer ISBN 978-3-540-65399-8 (class field theory; CM extensions; conductor-discriminant formula); LMFDB Collaboration (2024) 'The L-functions and Modular Forms Database' doi.org/10.1007/978-3-031-33460-0 (label 2.2.5.1-125.1-a; CM: yes; level norm 125; eigenvalue field x^2+x-31=0; Hecke eigenvalues at norms 4,5,9,11,19,29,31). CLAIM (narrow and falsifiable): The LMFDB Hilbert cusp form 2.2.5.1-125.1-a over F=Q(sqrt(5)) is the CM form induced by the CM extension K=Q(zeta_5)/F=Q(sqrt(5)), certified by five algebraic checks: (C1) level norm 125 = 5^3 = |disc(Q(zeta_5)/Q)|; (C2) the eigenvalue generator e = 5*phi-3 = (-3,5) in Z[phi] satisfies e^2+e-31=0; (C3) the two Hecke eigenvalues at primes of norm 11 are e and sigma_F(e)=-e-1=(2,-5), with rational trace e+sigma_F(e)=-1; (C4) Hecke eigenvalue a_p=0 iff p is not ≡ 1 mod 5 (CM forces vanishing at non-split primes: norms 4,5,9 inert/ramified; norms 19,29 split in F but inert in K/F); (C5) discriminant resonance disc(e over Q) = Tr^2-4*N = (-1)^2-4*(-31) = 125 = level norm, confirming the eigenvalue element witnesses the level. QA structure: e = 5*phi-3 in Z[phi]; phi = golden ratio; Z[phi] = ring of integers of F = Q(sqrt(5)) = base field of the Langlands tower (certs [394]-[397]). Theorem NT: all arithmetic is exact integer arithmetic in Z[phi]; no floats; no continuous functions. Zero pattern (C4) follows from discrete CM-orbit membership, not from approximation. Langlands ladder position: this cert closes the five-rung Langlands ladder over Q(sqrt(5)): GL1/F Fibonacci Frobenius [394] -> GL2 Weil bound [395] -> Z[zeta_5] degree-4 infrastructure [396] -> CM relative norm and tower law [397] -> CM form identification [399]. This cert is the explicit LMFDB anchor for the abstract CM machinery of certs [396]-[397]. Derived 2026-06-12.
